mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in less than 55% of months with positive PAYEMS momentum over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% reflects restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates applied to equity cash flows and tightens financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in less than 45% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of falling retail sales and rising unemployment jointly signals consumer demand collapse and labor market weakening, producing strongly negative 60-day equity returns that neither variable alone captures.
failure_condition: The interaction term of RSAFS and UNRATE does not add incremental predictive power (delta adjusted R-squared < 0.01) over main effects in a 3-year rolling regression predicting 60-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, industrial production growth slows as capacity constraints bind; negative momentum in INDPRO forecasts a shift to contraction and negative 60-day equity returns.
failure_condition: Triggered signals produce positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts reflects durable demand growth and construction sector momentum, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not positive in more than 55% of months with rising HOUST over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with a flat or falling federal funds rate indicates negative real policy rates, which stimulate short-term nominal growth and risk appetite, producing positive 5-day equity returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change > 3% while FEDFUNDS unchanged or lower
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to produce positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over 3 months signals labor market deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades and negative 20-day equity returns.
failure_condition: Triggered instances do not produce negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth surprises, captured by quarter-over-quarter changes in GDPC1, predict positive equity returns as they reflect aggregate demand expansion not yet fully priced into the market.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following positive GDPC1 surprises are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.
